name: Preserver Activist ("Preevert")
description: Preserver Youth or Activist Group vigilante — swaggering, poorly trained armed thugs who intimidate dissenters after Dark Day. Well-armed but rarely travel in groups smaller than four; call corrupt police when losing.
packageNotes: Section: Enemies & Minor NPCs (pp. 199–202). GM-only encounter archetype — not player creation.
Favor pickup trucks, jeeps, and off-road vehicles even in cities.
Police (often non-human) side with Preeverts against their victims.

numberAppearing.notes: Rarely travel in groups of less than four.

handToHand.notes: Hand to Hand: Basic; two attacks per melee.

weaponProficiencies.notes: One ancient W.P. — blunt or knife.
One ancient W.P. — blunt or knife.
One or two modern W.P.s — revolver, pistol, or rifle.

horrorFactorMorale.notes: Firefight or Morphus Nightbane confrontation — fail save: flee or surrender (flee always first choice).

dispositionNotes: Dress: white T-shirts with Preserver slogan "Save America for Americans," leather jackets, black jeans, combat boots; crew cuts or shaved heads.
Bullies — mock, insult, and push those they perceive as weaker; respectful and obedient to authority.
Not used to dangerous confrontation despite carrying weapons.

name: Nightbane Gang Member
description: Golden Posse, Warlords, or street gang hardened criminal — inner-city veteran; bullies like Preeverts but hates them; Warlord gangs may know Nightbane and occasionally include Nightbane backers.
packageNotes: Occasionally one or two appear alone; similar gangs may include vampires, Wampyrs, or other supernaturals as members/leaders.
Warlord gangs: 10% Nightbane backing if 2–6 members (1D4+2); 15% if 7+ members.
Hates Preserver Activists — shoot on sight.

composition.notes: Either P.S. 17 or P.P. 17; all other attributes average.

attributeNotes: Either P.S. 17 or P.P. 17; all others average.

levelOfExperience.notes: 1 in 5 are 3rd level with higher combat bonuses.

weaponProficiencies.notes: One archaic W.P. — blunt, chain, or knife.
One archaic W.P. — blunt, chain, or knife.
One archaic W.P. — blunt, chain, or knife.
Two modern W.P.s — automatic pistol, automatic rifle, or sub-machinegun.

horrorFactorMorale.notes: Actual combat vs dangerous enemies — fail: flee. Warlord gangs less likely to flee from Nightbane (+1 save vs Nightbane H.F.).

dispositionNotes: Swaggering bullies — loud music, graffiti tagging, street intimidation.
Golden Posse favors yellow; Warlords use speckled red gang colors (bandannas/jackets).
Used to attacking surprised victims — less comfortable when targets fight back.

name: Corrupted Police
description: Post–Dark Day Nightlord-aligned police — fanatical cultists, criminals, or corrupt officers; human or Doppleganger replacements. Call backup rapidly; SWAT and NSB for serious threats. Good cops exist as rare minority (see disposition).
packageNotes: Typical squad car: 2 officers; 2D6 additional officers in 1D6 minutes when confronted.
Detectives work in pairs or groups of 3–6 — half may be Dopplegangers, Hollow Men, or other minion disguises.
Add +1 level of experience for detectives and ranking officers.
Sideline crimes: protection, drugs, extortion, murder/robbery of Nightbane and dissidents.

composition.notes: One attribute at 17 (typically P.S., P.P., or P.E.); Dopplegangers have supernatural P.S.

numberAppearing.notes: Patrol pair per squad car; 2D6 backup in 1D6 minutes; detectives 2–6; SWAT/NSB for heavy threats.

alignmentNotes: Anarchist or any evil (default archetype). Good peacekeepers are a rare minority — see dispositionNotes.
attributeNotes: One attribute at 17 (P.S., P.P., or P.E. typical); Dopplegangers have supernatural P.S.

vitals.notes: Human patrol default — Doppleganger variant: 50 S.D.C.

levelOfExperience.notes: Bonuses below are for 1st-level patrol officers; +1 level for detectives and ranking officers.

dispositionNotes: Evil majority: rude, cold, intimidating — abuse "perps" and Nightbane; minimum courtesy to "solid citizens."
Rare good cops may curb abuses, let suspects escape, or secretly aid Spook Squad, Lightbringers, and trusted Nightbane — risk murder if exposed.
Many good officers resigned and work underground with resistance groups.

variants.label: Evil detective
variants.notes: Doppleganger detectives also receive +4 save vs Horror Factor.

name: Night Cultist
description: Human, non-mystical follower of a Cult of Night — brainwashed recruit serving a Priest of Night or other Nightlord agent. Participates in ritual magic energy donation but has no spell knowledge. Fanaticism can negate fear.
packageNotes: Small cult: 3D6 members; large cults up to ~100 including families.
Most recruits had low self-esteem or mental/social problems — optional Random Insanity Table at induction.
Leader may be Night Prince, Avatar, Doppleganger, Priest of Night, Sorcerer, evil Nightbane, or other shape-changer — pick from existing catalogs.

numberAppearing.notes: Small cult cell; larger cults up to ~100 members.

weaponProficiencies.notes: Hand to Hand: Basic and two Weapon Proficiencies (any).

equipment.label: Handguns and rifles
equipment.notes: Some cults arm members with modern small-arms.

dispositionNotes: Range from cheerful flower-offerers to dark-robed fanatics muttering over victims.
Insidious cultists may appear normal or successful — movers and shakers may be dupes.
Some lost souls may turn against masters if they learn the truth about the Nightlords; many simply do not care who they serve.

name: NSB Field Agent
description: National Security Bureau federal operative — default Doppleganger field agent with Martial Arts training and heavy armor. Overwhelming-force raids (12+ agents per suspect). Alternate bases: Ashmedai or Hollow Man (see race RCC rows).
packageNotes: Investigative team: 2 agents. Field team: 3D6. Major raid: up to 50 agents with heavy weapons and military vehicles.
Almost every NSB field agent is Doppleganger, Ashmedai, or Hollow Man — see `race_doppleganger`, `race_ashmedai`, `race_hollow_man` + paired `race_namtar`.
Armed confrontation usually lethal — fleeing is the best hope for targets.

composition.notes: Printed block assumes Doppleganger agent; swap base race for Ashmedai/Hollow Man and use that row's supernatural abilities.

numberAppearing.notes: 2 for investigative team; 3D6 field team; up to 50 on major raid (12+ per suspected target in area).

weaponProficiencies.notes: Beretta 95 machine pistol — 2D6, 20-round magazine, bursts/full auto.
M-16 or heavier weapons when situation demands.

dispositionNotes: Cold, distant, emotionless — "just the facts, ma'am" toward civilians.
Neither pleas nor threats affect them; kill witnesses who see too much.
Conservative dark suits and shades; raid gear: black body armor with white "NSB" marking.
Very rarely a Doppleganger may show mercy or turn against fellow agents.
